channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18982] The lmm_stripe_offset is 0 for a file which is created on mdt 2
item.description:
I did following –

sudo lfs mkdir -i 1 -c 1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev
sudo lfs setstripe -E 1M -L mdt -E -1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev <<< DOM attire set
sudo lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev
lfs migrate -m 2 /mnt/lustre/rajeev/
lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev
touch /mnt/lustre/rajeev/file_test
lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev/file_test
—
[root@test1-single-node tests]# sudo lfs mkdir -i 1 -c 1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev
[root@test1-single-node tests]# sudo lfs setstripe -E 1M -L mdt -E -1 /mnt/lustre/rajeev
[root@test1-single-node tests]# sudo lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev
/mnt/lustre/rajeev
lcm_layout_gen: 0
lcm_mirror_count: 1
lcm_entry_count: 2
lcme_id: N/A
lcme_mirror_id: N/A
lcme_flags: 0
lcme_extent.e_start: 0
lcme_extent.e_end: 1048576
stripe_count: 0 stripe_size: 1048576 pattern: mdt stripe_offset: -1

lcme_id: N/A
lcme_mirror_id: N/A
lcme_flags: 0
lcme_extent.e_start: 1048576
lcme_extent.e_end: EOF
stripe_count: 1 stripe_size: 1048576 pattern: raid0 stripe_offset: -1

[root@test1-single-node tests]# lfs migrate -m 2 /mnt/lustre/rajeev/
[root@test1-single-node tests]# lfs getdirstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev
lmv_stripe_count: 1 lmv_stripe_offset: 2 lmv_hash_type: crush,fixed
mdtidx FID[seq:oid:ver]
2 [0x280000400:0x6:0x0]
[root@test1-single-node tests]# touch /mnt/lustre/rajeev/file_test
[root@test1-single-node tests]# lfs getstripe /mnt/lustre/rajeev/file_test
/mnt/lustre/rajeev/file_test
lcm_layout_gen: 2
lcm_mirror_count: 1
lcm_entry_count: 2
lcme_id: 1
lcme_mirror_id: 0
lcme_flags: init
lcme_extent.e_start: 0
lcme_extent.e_end: 1048576
lmm_stripe_count: 0
lmm_stripe_size: 1048576
lmm_pattern: mdt
lmm_layout_gen: 0
lmm_stripe_offset: 0 <<<< this is zero

lcme_id: 2
lcme_mirror_id: 0
lcme_flags: 0
lcme_extent.e_start: 1048576
lcme_extent.e_end: EOF
lmm_stripe_count: 1
lmm_stripe_size: 1048576
lmm_pattern: raid0
lmm_layout_gen: 0
lmm_stripe_offset: -1

Is this the right behavior ? the lmm_stripe_offset for lcme_id 1 should be 2 or zero. Please let me know so that I can fix this behaviour
item.summary: The lmm_stripe_offset is 0 for a file which is created on mdt 2

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59252/
Subject: LU-18982 lov: Do not display lmm_stripe_offset for mdt file
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 08c1787f24bac10f979cc044dd393fae1dba1ea5

"Rajeev Mishra <rajeevm@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59252
Subject: LU-18982 lov: Do not display lmm_stripe_offset for mdt file
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e06cf4845cfa5ec6c031030d07f342b7d8ac669b
Andreas, Thanks for the answering the query. I assume I can push a fix where getstripe does not print "lmm_stripe_offset" when (pattern is mdt pattern && lcme_id ==1)

The lov_mds_md struct existed long before DoM was created, and this field has "come along for the ride". It is not possible for the DoM component to be on a different MDT from the inode itself, so it doesn't make sense to allow this field to be changed.

I could imagine that this field would not be printed with "lfs getstripe" for a DoM component?

just opening for clarification on my last comment. Thanks!!!
in short lmm_stripe_offset is always ost index is correct statement ?

Thank @Andreas for your prompt response. I had a slight doubt about whether it was an OST index, and my lack of confidence stemmed from the DOM pattern (mdt). I naturally assumed it would point to the MDT target.

Thank you for the clarification. However, I am still a little unclear on why it should be OST 0 when it seems like it should be pointing to the MDT. Could you perhaps elaborate on that reasoning?

Thanks again for your help!

The "lmm_stripe_offset" is the OST starting index of a file in the file layout, and is printed with "lfs getstripe -i" (for the last initialized component). This is unrelated to the MDT number returned by "lfs getstripe -m".
